sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Borussia Dortmund have shown strong form in their last 5 matches, securing 3 wins and suffering 2 losses, with a potent attack scoring 9 goals. In contrast, 1. FC Heidenheim are struggling, with no wins in their last 6 matches, managing only 1 draw and 4 losses, while conceding 12 goals. This disparity highlights Dortmund's dominance going into the match.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Borussia Dortmund have dominated 1. FC Heidenheim, winning both matches with scores of 2-0 and 2-1. Dortmund have consistently scored early and controlled possession, averaging 69.5% in these games. Heidenheim have struggled to create big chances against Dortmund's defense, which could pose a challenge today.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Dortmund, sitting 2nd in the Bundesliga with 42 points, boast a robust defense (least goals conceded at 17) and a clinical attack (38 goals scored). Heidenheim, languishing at 18th with just 13 points, have scored only 17 goals while conceding 42, reflecting their defensive frailties. These stats underline Dortmund's superiority in both attack and defense.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Injuries
sections.text: Dortmund's Nico Schlotterbeck has been in fine form, averaging a 7.8 rating over the last 5 matches with 2 goals, though they miss key midfielder Marcel Sabitzer. Heidenheim lack standout performers in recent games and are without defender Benedikt Gimber, further weakening their backline. Dortmund's depth and quality in attack, with players like Karim Adeyemi and Serhou Guirassy, should exploit these gaps.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Dortmund's 81% win probability per Sofascore, combined with their superior form, head-to-head record, and league standing, they are overwhelming favorites at Signal Iduna Park. Heidenheim's poor away form and inability to keep clean sheets (22 games without one) suggest they will struggle to contain Dortmund's attack. Expect a one-sided affair with Dortmund likely to secure a comfortable victory.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at Signal Iduna Park between Borussia Dortmund and 1. FC Heidenheim ended in a 1-1 draw, with both teams finding the net late in the half. Dortmund took the lead through Waldemar Anton just before the break, only for Heidenheim to equalize almost immediately through Julian Niehues. The game has been competitive, with Dortmund dominating possession and creating more opportunities, while Heidenheim have shown resilience and capitalized on their chances. Both sides have had their moments, setting the stage for an intriguing second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Borussia Dortmund have controlled much of the game, holding the majority of possession and generating a higher number of entries into the final third and penalty area. Their ability to create big chances has been evident, though they've struggled with finishing, missing a couple of key opportunities. On the other hand, 1. FC Heidenheim have been more clinical despite having less of the ball, matching Dortmund in big chances created and making the most of their limited attacking forays. Defensively, Dortmund have won more duels, but Heidenheim's backline has held firm under pressure.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Dortmund largely dictate the tempo, with sustained periods of attacking pressure, especially in the middle and latter stages of the half. They enjoyed several prolonged spells of dominance, pushing forward with intent and creating waves of attacks. Heidenheim, however, started brightly with some early momentum, showing threat before fading as Dortmund took over. Their late surge just before halftime proved crucial, as they managed to level the score. The momentum shifts suggest Dortmund are likely to press hard again after the break, but Heidenheim have shown they can strike on the counter.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Dortmund, Julian Brandt has been pivotal in midfield, creating opportunities with key passes and posing a constant threat. Serhou Guirassy, despite missing a big chance, remains a danger in the box and will be eager to make amends. Waldemar Anton, already on the scoresheet, has been a rock at the back while contributing offensively. On the Heidenheim side, Julian Niehues has been their standout, scoring the equalizer and showing composure in critical moments. Eren Dinkçi also remains a threat upfront, having come close to scoring in the first half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical insights
halftimeSections.text: Dortmund's high possession and frequent final third entries indicate a strategy focused on relentless pressure and breaking down Heidenheim's defense through sustained attacks. They may look to exploit the flanks more in the second half to stretch the opposition. Heidenheim, meanwhile, appear content to soak up pressure and hit on the break, a tactic that worked for their late first-half goal. They will likely prioritize defensive organization while seeking quick transitions to catch Dortmund off guard.
